reviews.review_description: I think it cured my cancer!

Honestly this game gets way too much hate. I first picked it up when it was still on SideQuest and not App Lab. The problem is with Quest O Rama is that as soon as people see it they immediately get displeased and just drop it after a couple sessions. Sure it definitely does have bugs and problems nor the best looks but after playing it for a while you realize that it gets better the more you play. Once you go down a few levels you'll start fighting bosses that are genuine tests to your strength. Also the secret cassettes and tapes that you can find give you a real "Aha!" moment. The bugs and glitches and overall performance could do better but it's a very promising game with lots of potential.
You can somehow know exactly what's happening without understanding any of it. It is...something.
I was so confused, and the terrible grapple physics didn’t help. It just wasn’t Anita me at all.

reviews.review_title: Don't know why QOR is disliked.
reviews.review_description: QOR is amazing but confusing at times. Give this game a try it's a lot of fun. Really the only confusing thing is the story but not every good game needs a story.

Got stuck at reloading the clip to weapon instruction at the start, reinstalled the game, deleted game files, still not working, then I jump right into the game skipping the instructions, have no idea what I am suppose to do.
